title: BPT `approve` is vulnerable to the standard allowance race
reason: Standard ERC20 allowance race condition; user-side approval workflow issue rather than a Balancer-specific protocol vulnerability.

title: BPT `transferFrom` emits an incorrect `Approval` event
reason: Off-chain event/accounting inconsistency only; it does not create an on-chain loss, lockup, or manipulation path.

title: Integer Overflow in bpow Approximation Function
reason: `bpowi()` uses checked `bmul()` arithmetic, so overflow reverts rather than silently corrupting pricing.

title: Unchecked External Call Return Value
reason: The contract does check the returned boolean, and state-changing entrypoints are guarded by `_lock_`; the non-returning-ERC20 compatibility issue is not a separate reportable exploit here.

title: Controller Can Manipulate Pool Parameters Arbitrarily
reason: Privileged controller behavior is an explicit trust assumption of the design, not a code vulnerability.

title: Potential Division by Zero in bdiv
reason: `bdiv()` explicitly checks `b != 0` and guards multiplication overflow; the stated silent-failure path is unsupported.

title: Insufficient Slippage Protection in swapExactAmountIn
reason: MEV/sandwich risk is not caused by a missing contract check here; users already supply `minAmountOut` and `maxPrice` bounds.

title: Token Balance Desynchronization During Joins
reason: If `transferFrom()` returns `false`, the `require` reverts and state rolls back; only the distinct 'returns true without transferring' case survives and is captured in F-002.

title: Deprecated floating point in increaseApproval
reason: `increaseApproval()` / `decreaseApproval()` use checked math and the claim does not identify a concrete exploitable path.
